note: Native chart specs for the research site, extracted from each experiment's own result files (runs/reports/analysis JSON+CSV and README/report tables) by per-experiment agent passes and kept only when every plotted value was located verbatim in the cited source by machine verification. Rendered by scripts/build_site.py (chart_svg/chart_figure); raw matplotlib figures remain available further down each experiment page.

charts.title: Final-answer accuracy on hardest held-out length (12 ops)
charts.note: ACK's dynamic weight edits beat its own ablations but do not beat an ordinary fixed recurrent controller at length 12.

charts.title: Final-answer accuracy vs program length
charts.note: All arms degrade steeply beyond the trained lengths; ACK tracks the fixed controller rather than exceeding it.

charts.title: Mechanism check: intermediate-state step accuracy at length 12
charts.note: Shuffling or randomizing the conditioning codes collapses ACK's state tracking, so the edits do ordered work - yet a plain GRU still tracks state better.

charts.title: Held-out operation compositions: final-answer accuracy
charts.note: On held-out op compositions ACK edges the fixed GRU at comp8 but the gap vanishes at comp12; no arm escapes the collapse.

charts.title: Seed spread at the hardest length (12 ops)
charts.note: Seed-to-seed variation at length 12 is as large as the ACK-vs-GRU gap, so neither recurrent arm reliably wins.

charts.title: Training curves: validation strict-pair accuracy (seed 101)
charts.note: ACK and the fixed GRU learn at nearly identical rates; disabling the dynamic weight edits stalls learning almost completely.

charts.title: Ordered vs shuffled vs random conditioning across program lengths
charts.note: Shuffling or randomizing the op-conditioning stream collapses state tracking at every length, so ACK's computation is genuinely ordered.

charts.title: Joint belief state vs controls: probability mass on correct states (mod 31)
charts.note: Only the joint recurrent filter exceeds 91% target mass at every length; both controls stay under 6%, so joint state and recurrence both matter.

charts.title: Execution threshold: target mass jumps once step budget K reaches program length L
charts.note: Mass stays under 14% while K<L and jumps above 91% exactly when K>=L, even at lengths 12-24 the model never saw in training (lengths 1-8).

charts.title: Before vs at the threshold: best mass with too few steps vs mass at K=L
charts.note: One extra step at the K=L boundary takes target mass from under 14% to over 91%; top-1-on-support hits 100% at the same point.

charts.title: Mid-training vs final: extra training tightens the belief state at every length
charts.x_label: Program length L (evaluated at K = L)
charts.note: The K=L threshold already exists at step 250; steps 250-500 mostly sharpen mass, with the biggest gain (+17pp) at the longest programs.

charts.title: Mod-11 pilot replicates the result: joint beats marginal and static controls
charts.x_label: Program length L (evaluated at K = L)
charts.note: The joint-vs-controls gap is not modulus-specific: at p=11 the joint model holds 92-96% mass while both controls stay under 18%.

charts.title: Why longer programs look easier: filtering shrinks the feasible state set

series.label: Mean support size (states consistent with observations)

charts.note: More observations collapse the support from 13 states to 1.7, so target NLL falls with length even though execution is longer.

charts.title: A handful of exact bridge examples unlocks recombination repair
charts.x_label: Exact bridge examples per held-out pair (dose)
charts.note: Holdout repair jumps from 6.7% to ~30% by dose 2-4, but heavy dosing (8) erodes seen-IID performance; dose 2 is the sweet spot.

charts.title: Near-miss examples do not substitute for exact bridge pairs
charts.note: A near-miss-heavy curriculum stays at 8.3%, near the 6.7% no-bridge baseline; only exact factor-pair bridges lift holdout repair to ~30%.

charts.title: Recombination gains require an aligned trace at train and test time
charts.note: Removing or shuffling the trace at training or prompt time collapses the dose-8 recombination gain back to the ~7-8% floor.

charts.title: Which withheld families the bridge dose actually unlocks
charts.note: The dose gain is concentrated: sorted_contains_count jumps to 91-100% while modulo_sum_label never repairs at any dose.

charts.title: Dose 8 holdout repair varies hugely by primitive factor
charts.x_label: Primitive factor in the withheld pair
charts.note: Iteration and string-normalization factors repair at 91.7% while modulo stays at 0%; tuple_access (12.5%) omitted to fit 10 bars.

charts.title: Bridge dose has a cost: seen-task accuracy drops at dose 8
charts.note: Higher doses displace seen-combination training data: seen-IID repair falls from 86.1% to 58.3% while format-shift robustness holds.

charts.title: Repair success: only trace-trained SFT with real traces repairs the bug
charts.note: Trace SFT with real counterexample traces hits 91% IID and 53% on format holdout; every control stays near zero, and no condition transfers to the withheld rule family.
charts.source: report table (reports/counterexample_rule_repair_paper.md, Final Results); matches reports/final_core_results.csv

charts.title: Trace adapter ablation: the trace input, not the adapter alone, carries the signal
charts.note: The same trace-trained adapter drops from 91% to 0% when the failed-test trace is removed or shuffled: it is reading the counterexamples, not memorizing patches.
charts.source: report table (reports/counterexample_rule_repair_paper.md, Trace Adapter Ablations); matches reports/final_ablation_results.csv

charts.title: Trace SFT repair rate by rule family
charts.note: Format-holdout transfer varies by family (threshold rules fully transfer, affine ones collapse); the withheld parity_offset family is 0/45 everywhere.
charts.source: report table (reports/counterexample_rule_repair_paper.md, Trace Adapter Family Breakdown); matches reports/final_trace_by_family.csv

charts.title: Patch format vs actual repair: SFT fixes syntax, only traces fix semantics
charts.note: Every SFT variant learns to emit perfectly applicable patches, but only trace-trained SFT turns applicable patches into correct repairs.

charts.title: Training budget matters: 90 examples x 1 epoch vs 240 x 3 epochs
charts.note: A 90-example one-epoch pilot already reaches 45% repair; scaling to 240 examples, 3 epochs, rank 32 nearly doubles it to 85%.

charts.title: Held-out length 24 (mod 31): only cyclic-structured transitions stay exact
charts.note: Generic and Fourier MLP transitions collapse at length 24, while cyclic mixer and primitive router match the exact-rule ceiling at 100%.
charts.source: report table (reports/cyclic_transition_ladder_paper.md, Main Results; matches analysis/first_k_ge_l_summary.csv)

charts.title: Query mass vs program length (mod 31 main sweep)
charts.note: Dense MLP variants degrade steadily with length (training saw lengths 1-8); structured transitions generalize exactly to 3x the training horizon.
charts.source: report table (reports/cyclic_transition_ladder_paper.md, Main Results; matches analysis/first_k_ge_l_summary.csv)

charts.title: Scale check: structured transitions stay near-exact at modulus 97
charts.y_label: strict belief target mass at length 24
charts.note: Tripling the residue space barely dents the structured learners: 99.8-99.9% belief mass at length 24, with 100% route accuracy.
charts.source: report tables (reports/cyclic_transition_ladder_paper.md, Main Results and Modulus-97 Scale Check)

charts.title: Soft routing stays slightly imperfect while hard routing is exact
charts.note: The cyclic mixer's soft routing misroutes ~5-10% at small moduli yet still reads out exactly; the hard router is always at 1.0.

charts.title: Dense recurrent executor vs static compiler: exact query mass by program length (mod 31)
charts.note: The recurrent dense state does real sequential work, beating a one-pass compiler at every length, but is far from exact at long programs.
charts.source: analysis/summary.md (controls table, from runs/*/metrics_final.csv); report table

charts.title: Compute threshold: query mass jumps when internal steps K reach program length L (mod 31)
charts.note: Each length stays flat until K reaches L, then jumps: extra internal steps only help when they consume more program instructions.

charts.title: Belief-probe audit: recoverable belief mass in the hidden state (mod 31)
charts.note: A frozen post-hoc probe recovers far more belief information from the recurrent state, but even it decays fast — no high-fidelity belief machine.
charts.source: analysis/summary.md (controls table, from runs/*/metrics_final.csv); report table

charts.title: Small-modulus diagnostic (mod 11): instruction order matters
charts.note: Shuffling instruction order before execution erases most of the recurrent advantage, so the gain comes from genuinely sequential processing.

charts.title: Which query is hardest? Query mass at the compute threshold, by query type (mod 31)
charts.x_label: Program length L (evaluated at first K >= L)
charts.note: Single-variable queries (A, B) beat the combined A+B and A-B queries at every length; the gap persists at held-out lengths.

charts.title: Training curves (mod 31): executor query mass, then post-hoc belief probe
charts.note: The executor climbs from 38% to ~69% query mass; a probe on the frozen hidden state then recovers up to 64% belief mass within 2000 steps.

charts.title: Small-modulus (mod 11) belief audit: only the ordered recurrent model keeps belief at held-out lengths
charts.x_label: Program length L (trained on lengths up to 6)
charts.note: At held-out lengths 9 and 12 the recurrent model keeps 31-39% recoverable belief while both controls collapse below 6%.

charts.title: Top-1 accuracy on the true support (mod 31): dense recurrent vs static compiler
charts.note: By top-1 accuracy the recurrent model roughly doubles-to-quintuples the static compiler, and the gap widens at held-out lengths 16 and 24.

charts.title: Query accuracy by supervision strength (modulus 31)
charts.note: With architecture fixed, richer training targets win at every length: full belief supervision roughly doubles sampled-label accuracy at L=24.

charts.title: Is the belief state decodable at length 24? (modulus 31)
charts.note: Only belief-distilled conditions yield a working belief decoder at scale; full belief supervision gives the most recoverable state.
charts.source: experiments/dense_supervision_ladder/analysis/summary.md (Modulus 31 Ladder at K=L table, L=24 rows)

charts.title: Recurrent budget matters: full-belief accuracy with K=L vs best K<L
charts.note: Performance jumps when the recurrent budget K reaches the program length, but accuracy still degrades with length even under full supervision.

charts.title: Pilot (modulus 11) shows the same supervision ordering
charts.note: The smaller modulus-11 diagnostic reproduces the ladder: prefix targets are a big jump and full belief distillation is best overall.

charts.title: Accuracy by query type at length 24: sums and differences are hardest
charts.note: Direct reads of A or B beat arithmetic queries for every supervision; the supervision ordering holds across all four query types.

charts.title: Linear-probe decodability of the belief state across sequence length
charts.x_label: Sequence length L (evaluated at K = L)
charts.note: Probe decodability decays with length for every supervision, but full-belief keeps the largest readable margin at every L, not just L = 24.

charts.title: The task itself sharpens with length: candidate answer sets shrink
charts.y_label: Mean support size (candidates consistent with observations)
charts.note: By L = 24 only ~1.5-1.6 answers remain consistent on average, so falling accuracy at long lengths reflects harder inference, not more options.

charts.title: Best dense student: projected query mass vs exact belief mass (mod 31)
charts.note: The best model (residual MLP, width 512) tracks queries far better than the exact belief, so it learned a compressed approximation, not exact execution.

charts.title: Projected query mass by program length across main variants (mod 31)
charts.note: Doubling state width from 256 to 512 gives the biggest gain; the residual transition adds a smaller consistent edge over the GRU.

charts.title: Exact pair-belief mass by program length across main variants (mod 31)
charts.note: Strict belief mass collapses with length for every variant: wider states help but no dense student becomes an exact belief executor.

charts.title: Pilot (mod 11): decoder and width ablation at train length 6
charts.note: The low-rank decoder is clearly worse at representing correlated pair beliefs, so it was dropped from the main sweep.

charts.title: Frozen linear probe vs trained decoder: belief mass at K = L (best variant, mod 31)
charts.note: The frozen probe matches the trained decoder at every length: no hidden exact belief exists in the state, so the state itself is the bottleneck.

charts.title: Which components must be structured? Strict belief mass at held-out length 24 (mod 31)
charts.note: The fully learned structured executor (98.1%) nearly matches the oracle; swapping either part for an unstructured one collapses performance.

charts.title: Length generalization: strict belief mass vs held-out program length (mod 31, trained on lengths 1-8)
charts.note: The learned structured executor stays near oracle out to 3x training length; unstructured init or transition degrades steeply with length.

charts.title: Scale check at modulus 97: learned executor vs oracle over held-out length
charts.note: At modulus 97 the learned executor still reaches 94.9% at length 24; the small gap comes from slow belief-mass diffusion, not routing errors.

charts.title: Training curves at modulus 31: answer mass over optimizer steps
charts.note: Sinkhorn-init routers climb past 97% by step ~600, while generic-MLP init and MLP transitions plateau near 60-80%.

charts.title: Where learned variants fail at modulus 31: initial belief vs final answer at length 24
charts.note: Generic-MLP init already loses ~41% mass at step 0, while the MLP transition erodes a good Sinkhorn init from 58.9% to 19.9%.
charts.source: analysis/summary.md table (First K >= L Summary, main modulus 31, length 24)

charts.title: Replication at modulus 11: answer mass vs held-out program length
charts.note: The mod-31 ordering replicates at modulus 11: structured init plus router stays above 99% while MLP components decay with length.
charts.source: analysis/summary.md table (First K >= L Summary, pilot modulus 11)

charts.title: Learning-rate ablation at modulus 7: answer mass at length 3
charts.note: Raising the learning rate to 0.1 lifts the smoke-phase router from 87.1% to 99.4%; the cyclic mixer improves less.
charts.source: analysis/summary.md table (First K >= L Summary, smoke modulus 7, length 3)

charts.title: Final-probe accuracy at 2 TTT steps: true observations vs corrupted controls
charts.note: No arm separates from 25% four-choice chance; the best score (30.6%) is a corrupted control, so the mechanism fails its gate.

charts.title: Accuracy vs number of TTT steps (in-distribution eval)
charts.note: More per-episode gradient steps never lift true-observation TTT above chance; the only gain shows up in a corrupted control.

charts.title: Held-out observation cross-entropy after TTT (held-out family split)
charts.note: TTT lowers held-out observation loss, but corrupted-observation updates lower it just as much, so the CE drop is not episode-grounded.

charts.title: Per-seed accuracy at 2 TTT steps (held-out family split)
charts.note: Seed 101 drives all arm separation; seeds 202 and 303 sit at 25% chance, so apparent echo gains are within seed noise.

charts.title: Held-out observation CE reduction after 2 TTT steps, by update content
charts.note: Corrupted-observation updates cut held-out CE about as much as true ones, so the CE gain is not grounded in true episode dynamics.

charts.title: Accuracy vs TTT steps on held-out families
charts.note: On families never seen in prefix training, extra TTT steps leave echo near 25% chance while shuffled updates drift below it.

charts.title: Global prefix training: validation observation CE by step
charts.note: Global prefix training is unstable: seed 101 briefly halves validation CE mid-run then regresses, and no seed ends better than it started.

charts.title: Repair success collapses on held-out bug families (Qwen3-4B)
charts.note: Repair-conditioned adapters solve 100% of same-family bugs but 0% of unseen families — template memorization, not transferable repair.

charts.title: Coding-specialist ablation: tiny held-out transfer, same IID saturation (Qwen2.5-Coder-3B)
charts.note: The coder model recovers only 3/27 held-out repairs with traces vs 2/27 without — one success above the no-trace control, too weak to credit traces.

charts.title: Trace-prompt ablation on held-out families: patches apply but nothing repairs
charts.note: Every prompt ablation repairs 0/27 held-out bugs even when most patches apply cleanly — execution traces show no causal benefit.
charts.source: reports/execution_conditioned_repair_paper.md (table: v2 Trace Adapter on v2 Held-Out Families)

charts.title: More training buys memorization, not transfer (Qwen3-4B trace adapter)
charts.note: Extending training from 3 to 12 epochs lifts train-set repair from 15% to 95%, yet held-out repair stays at exactly zero.

charts.title: On new bug families, repair SFT learns to game the visible test (Qwen3-4B, v2 held-out)

series.label: Passes visible test, fails hidden tests

charts.note: Every applied trace-adapter patch on held-out families passes the visible test but fails hidden tests — overfitting, not repair.

charts.title: Sampling 3 candidates does not rescue held-out repair (Qwen3-4B, v2)
charts.note: At temperature 0.2 with 3 samples per episode, patches still apply but repair@3 is 0 for every condition — more samples buy nothing.
charts.source: report table (reports/execution_conditioned_repair_paper.md, v2 held-out-family best-of-3 results)

charts.title: v1 pilot: SFT fixed diff formatting, never semantics (Qwen3-4B, 14 held-out episodes)
charts.y_label: Rate on v1 held-out synthetic set
charts.note: In the first pilot, SFT raised patch applicability from 7% to 71% while repair success stayed at zero across all conditions.
charts.source: report table (reports/execution_conditioned_repair_paper.md, v1 held-out synthetic results)

charts.title: Repair success by training condition and evaluation split
charts.note: Trace training lifts seen and format-shifted repair to 64-81%, but held-out factor recombination stays near 8-10% for every condition.

charts.title: Trace-trained adapter with traces removed or shuffled at test time
charts.note: The trace-trained adapter collapses without its correct trace, so the model is genuinely reading trace content, not ignoring it.

charts.title: Recombination holdout by held-out bug family
charts.note: Almost all holdout success comes from one family (sorted_tuple_affine); most novel factor pairings never succeed.

charts.title: Recombination holdout by shared factor (trace-trained adapter)
charts.note: Held-out recombination stays near zero for most factors; only ordering and tuple_access show any transfer.
charts.source: reports/factor_recombination_ladder_report.md (Trace Ladder Holdout By Factor table; length factor at 0.0% omitted for the 10-category limit)

charts.title: Factor-labelled adapter: removing labels barely hurts, removing traces collapses it
charts.note: Stripping factor labels costs only ~3-6 points, so the adapter leans on trace content, not the labels themselves.

charts.title: Visible vs hidden test pass rate on seen combinations
charts.note: Hidden-test pass rates match or slightly exceed visible ones, so successful repairs are not overfitting the shown tests.

charts.title: LoRA training cost per condition
charts.note: All four adapters cost about seven minutes to train, so the big accuracy gaps come from trace content, not compute.

charts.title: Repair success by training coverage and eval split
charts.note: Each coverage recipe masters its own distribution, but all three land at ~20-23% on held-out recombinations — mixing buys no extra transfer.

charts.title: Aligned traces are necessary: mixed-coverage controls on recombination holdout
charts.note: Removing or corrupting traces at train or eval time collapses recombination repair from 21.7% to near zero.

charts.title: Recombination transfer is concentrated in one family
charts.note: All the apparent recombination transfer comes from the sorted-join family (12/12 for every adapter); the other four families stay near zero.

charts.title: Traces must also be present at evaluation time, not just in training
charts.note: Removing or shuffling the trace at eval time collapses the trained mixed adapter to near zero on every split.

charts.title: Mixed adapter's holdout transfer is confined to a few primitive factors
charts.note: Three factors transfer perfectly while most sit near zero; the length factor (0.0%, omitted) also fails, so transfer is factor-specific.

charts.title: Holdout failures are semantic, not formatting: patches apply but tests fail
charts.note: Every condition emits well-formed, applicable patches on the holdout, so the 20-point gap to hidden-test pass is pure rule misunderstanding.

charts.title: Belief-state execution (mod 31): joint recurrent executor vs controls
charts.note: Only joint latent state executes correlated beliefs: marginal and static controls sit at the 3.2% (1/31) baseline, even on held-out lengths.

charts.title: Target-support mass vs internal steps K: sharp threshold at K = program length
charts.note: Mass stays near baseline until K reaches program length, then jumps to 87-98% — the executor signature, generalizing past training lengths.

charts.title: Before vs at the K=L threshold (joint executor, mod 31)
charts.note: Extra internal steps only help when they correspond to executing the program: sub-threshold budgets stay near the 3.2% baseline at every length.

charts.title: Threshold quality scales across moduli: support mass at K = L
charts.y_label: Target-support mass at first K >= L
charts.note: At both moduli, support mass at the K=L threshold decays only gently with program length while top-1 stays at 100%.

charts.title: Training curves (mod 31 belief task): only the joint executor learns
charts.note: The joint executor hits 100% train pair accuracy by step 100; the marginal control never leaves baseline and the static control plateaus low.

charts.title: Why the belief task is needed: exact-state pilot does not separate models (mod 11, L=12)
charts.note: On single-state programs the marginal control also reaches 100% at K=L, so only the correlated belief task forces joint state.

charts.title: Exact answer accuracy vs internal recurrent steps (modulo-97, held-out lengths 12-24)
charts.note: Accuracy is near zero until the internal step budget K reaches program length L, then jumps to 100% -- even for lengths never seen in training.

charts.title: Structured executor vs controls at full step budget (modulo-31)
charts.note: Recurrence alone is not enough: without the categorical register state, both controls stay near chance while the structured executor is perfect.

charts.title: Training convergence: structured executor vs controls
charts.note: The categorical executor hits 100% train accuracy within 150 steps; recurrence without the structured state never leaves chance.

charts.title: Extra latent steps buy nothing without structured state (unstructured control, mod 31)
charts.note: Sweeping the step budget on the generic recurrent control is flat at ~0-1%: recurrence alone gives no test-time-compute scaling.

charts.title: Partial computation ramps before the exact answer snaps in (length-4 programs)
charts.note: Register-level accuracy climbs with each latent step below the K=L threshold, showing the model is genuinely executing partway.

charts.title: K=L step threshold replicates on modulo-31, including trained lengths
charts.note: On a second modulus the same sharp rule holds for trained and held-out lengths alike: chance until K reaches L, then exactly 100%.

charts.title: Modulus 31: learned components fall far below the exact oracle ceiling (query mass vs program length)
charts.note: The slot format can represent exact belief (ceiling = 100%), but learned initializers and neural transitions both degrade with length at modulus 31.

charts.title: Modulus 31 strict belief mass: neural transitions collapse hardest
charts.note: On the stricter belief metric the neural transition drops to 12.3% at length 24; the learned initializer with exact transitions holds 36.5%.

charts.title: Modulus 11 pilot: neural transitions are learnable when slots start from oracle initialization
charts.note: The one clear positive: with oracle slots, a learned transition holds 97% query mass out to held-out length 12; end-to-end learning composes worst.

charts.title: Held-out belief mass at the longest evaluated length: pilot success does not scale
charts.note: The same neural transition that reaches 95.5% belief mass at modulus 11 falls to 12.3% at modulus 31 - a scale failure, not a representation failure.

charts.title: Slot memory purity degrades with program length (modulus 31)
charts.note: Every variant loses slot purity as programs lengthen; neural transitions plateau near 0.31 while others keep decaying.

charts.title: Training progress on modulus 31: query mass climbs but never converges
charts.note: Both modulus-31 runs are still improving at budget end, but neural transitions lag exact transitions by roughly 10 points throughout.

charts.title: Modulus 11 ablation: supervision and initialization both matter
charts.note: Only oracle-initialized slots hold query mass across length; learning init and transitions together decays fastest (89% to 58%).

charts.title: Smoke test at modulus 7: neural transitions already lag at length 3

charts.title: Query accuracy on held-out program lengths: joint belief state vs controls
charts.note: Only the joint recurrent model keeps ~96%+ query accuracy out to length 24, three times its training length; both controls collapse.

charts.title: Execution threshold: query accuracy jumps exactly when step budget K reaches program length L

charts.title: Hidden belief state becomes accurate without ever being supervised

charts.title: Below the threshold, compound queries are hardest to guess

charts.title: The second half of training mainly sharpens long-program answers
